fact: March 5 is exactly 64 days into the year - that's the same number as squares on a chess board!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is prime time for maple trees to start dripping their sweet sap that becomes syrup - some trees can produce 10 gallons of sap in a single day!
Why do cats get extra crazy around now? March 5 falls right in the middle of 'kitten season' when outdoor cats start having babies because daylight hours are getting longer!
The sun rises about 2 minutes earlier each day around March 5 in most places - by the end of the week, you'll gain 14 whole minutes of daylight!
March 5 sits smack in the middle of Pisces season, making it one of the most 'watery' days of the year according to astrology - no wonder spring rains love this time!
Earthworms go absolutely bonkers around this date! Warming soil makes them super active, and you can find hundreds wiggling around after any March rain.
In Antarctica, March 5 marks the beginning of serious darkness - the sun starts setting earlier each day as winter approaches down south!
This date falls exactly 16 days before the spring equinox, putting it in the perfect 'almost spring' sweet spot when nature can't decide if it's winter or spring!
Frogs in ponds start their loud, croaky love songs around March 5 in warmer places - some can be heard from over a mile away!
The moon phase on March 5 changes every year, but when it's a full moon on this date, it's called a 'Worm Moon' because that's when earthworms start popping up everywhere!
March 5 is typically one of the windiest days of the year in many places - perfect for flying kites but terrible for fancy hairdos!
Dandelions are secretly preparing their yellow flower bombs around this time - their roots are storing up energy underground for the big spring explosion!
